name: @othmanadi/loophole-extension
description: Loophole Kit: the Ableton extension shell (.ablx) that starts the Loophole Bridge and registers all five context-menu extensions. SDK-facing and private; built locally against the licensee's own Ableton Extensions SDK.
author: Ahmad Othman Ammar Adi (CodingWithAdi)
comment:scripts: typecheck/lint/test/build are the CI-safe gates: they run with NO Ableton SDK present (the beta SDK is non-redistributable, never committed; ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md §1/§3). The CI tsconfig + eslint config exclude every SDK-importing file, and `build` is a deliberate no-op placeholder so `pnpm -r build` stays green SDK-free. The `*:live` scripts are LOCAL-ONLY: they require the licensee to install the SDK + CLI tarballs first (`pnpm add file:<sdk>.tgz` and the cli .tgz; see README), and to copy tsconfig.live.example.json to the gitignored tsconfig.live.json. None of those local changes are committed.

scripts.build: node -e "console.log('loophole-extension: CI build is a no-op placeholder; the real .ablx bundle is the local-only build:live / package:live scripts')"
